name: TransactionalEmailDelivery
concept: Queue and deliver transactional email (password reset, user creation, notifications)

observed_steps.step: 1. Service calls IEmailService.Save with email parameters
2. Save() delegates to SendEmailMessages() with single recipient
3. Create EmailMessage record with Status=Importing
4. SaveEmailMessage() persists to database
5. Update Status to Queued

source.note: Inferred from status lifecycle - exact line not verified

observed_steps.step: 6a. If sendFast=true: Push to ISingleSendGridProcessingChannel

source.note: sendFast parameter passed but channel push not visible in excerpt

observed_steps.step: 6b. Else: EmailBackgroundService polling loop picks up queued message

trigger: Any system-initiated email: password reset, user creation, approval notification, etc.

rethink.problem: Dual dispatch path (sendFast vs polling) complicates flow
rethink.observed: sendFast=true pushes to channel, else background service polls
rethink.proposal: Single async queue with priority (urgent emails processed first)

name: BackgroundEmailProcessing
concept: Background worker polls channel and dispatches batches to gateway processor

observed_steps.step: 1. Background service waits for messages in channel
2. Check DisableAllBroadcasts setting
3. Dequeue batch of SmsLogIds (max 500 per batch)
4. For each SmsLogId: GetMessageId() from channel
5. Pass batch to EmailMessageWorkloadProcessor.ProcessWorkAsync()
6. For each email: ProcessEmailMessage() validates and sends
7. Validate email address (non-empty, valid format)
8. Update SmsLogs status to Sent

normalized_interpretation.Simplification: Event-driven queue consumer (RabbitMQ, Azure Service Bus)

trigger: Continuous background service polling (runs while app is running)

rethink.problem: Polling-based worker queries DB every N seconds (inefficient)
rethink.observed: DoWorkAsync() polls channel in while loop
rethink.proposal: Event-driven message queue (Azure Service Bus) with pub/sub

name: SendGridDelivery

observed_steps.step: 1. Query EmailMessages WHERE Status=Queued ORDER BY PriorityOrder

source.note: Referenced in behavior analysis but exact query line not confirmed

observed_steps.step: 2. For each EmailMessage: Read body and attachments from DB

observed_steps.step: 3. Call SendGrid API via HttpClient (POST to SendGrid)

source.note: Inferred from behavior - exact line not verified

observed_steps.step: 4a. On success: Update Status=SentToGateway, store XmessageId from SendGrid response

source.note: Inferred from behavior - exact line not verified

observed_steps.step: 4b. On failure: Update Status=SentToGatewayFailed or queue retry (QueuedFirstRetry/QueuedSecondRetry)

name: SendGridWebhookProcessing
concept: Receive SendGrid delivery status webhooks and update EmailMessage status

observed_steps.step: 1. InboundParseEventHandler receives POST /api/inbound from SendGrid
2. Parse SendGrid event type from webhook payload

source.note: Method exists but exact parsing logic line not verified

observed_steps.step: 3. Map SendGrid event type to EmailStatus enum (Delivered, Opened, etc.)

source.note: Behavior described but exact mapping line not verified

observed_steps.step: 4. Call UpdateEmailMessageStatusesAsync() with new status
5. Enforce no-downgrade rule: Delivered→Opened ignored, Delivered→Delivered ignored (DefaultNoChangeStatuses)

normalized_interpretation.Pattern: Webhook HTTP POST handler with status mapping
normalized_interpretation.Simplification: Typed webhook event processor (SendGridWebhookEvent) with FSM validation

name: Email2SmsGateway
concept: Parse inbound email and trigger SMS send if sender is whitelisted

observed_steps.step: 1. InboundParseEventHandler receives inbound email POST from SendGrid
2. Query Email2SmsWhitelistEntries WHERE Email = sender AND ProfileId = target profile

source.note: Behavior described but exact query line not verified

observed_steps.step: 3. If sender is whitelisted: Parse email body to extract message
4. Trigger SMS send via SmsService
5. If sender not whitelisted: Reject (no SMS sent)

trigger: Inbound email POST from SendGrid (Email2SMS use case)
note: Flow inferred from behavior documentation. InboundParseEventHandler handles BOTH status webhooks AND Email2SMS - mixed concerns.

rethink.problem: Two unrelated concerns in one handler (status updates + Email2SMS)
rethink.observed: InboundParseEventHandler processes both delivery status webhooks AND Email2SMS gateway messages
